source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
T2: Thread around a cylinder

One end of a thread is tied into a loop of length $L>2 \pi R$, and a cylinder of radius $R$ is put through the loop. The coefficient of friction between the thread and the cylinder is $\mu$. The free end of the thread is being pulled parallel to the axis of the cylinder (as shown by arrow in

<image_1>
the photo) while keeping the cylinder at rest. If the length of the loop is longer than a critical value, $L>L_{0}$, the loop can slide along the cylinder without changing its shape, otherwise the friction "locks" it into a place and increasing the pulling force would eventually just break the thread. Find this critical value $L_{0}$. The weight of the thread is to be neglected; the thread will not twist when being pulled.

It might be useful to know that

$$
2 \int \sqrt{1+x^{2}} \mathrm{~d} x=x \sqrt{1+x^{2}}+\operatorname{arcsinh} x,
$$

where $\operatorname{arcsinh} x \equiv \ln \left(x+\sqrt{1+x^{2}}\right)$.
extraction_notes: answer field is option label "A", mapped to the corresponding option text
image field contains a PIL image object placeholder rather than a path or URL, so image_paths is empty

issues: metadata.image_paths is empty, and metadata.extraction_notes says the original image field contained a PIL image object placeholder rather than a path or URL.

image_dependency: 题干明确写有“as shown by the arrow in the photo”，拉力方向与装置几何关系依赖图片说明。
输入字段给出 requires_image=true 且 text_dominant=false，表明该题不是纯文本主导。
题目涉及线绕圆柱的空间构型、接触形状与受力方向，属于明显的视觉/空间关系依赖。
multi_step: 要求求临界长度 L0，不是直接读图，需要结合几何约束、摩擦条件和连续受力关系推导。
题干提供积分提示，暗示解题过程包含非平凡数学推演，而非单步代入。
该题同时结合图示装置理解与物理/数学推导，具备多步多模态潜力。
verifiability: answer_type=short_text，标准答案为明确的符号表达式“2 π R”，结果形式清晰。
题目目标是单一临界值，最终答案可与候选表达式直接比对，便于规则或人工核验。
registry_passed=true，题干、答案和图像资产均已登记存在，基础核验条件较好。

risk_flags: choices contain duplicated option text (A and C are both 2 π R), which may reduce choice-based validation value.
metadata.image_paths is empty, and metadata.extraction_notes says the original image field contained a PIL image object placeholder rather than a path or URL.

decision_reasons: 题干、答案和主图像资产均已登记存在，核心语义支撑完整。
初步评分显示图像依赖性、高可验证性和较强多步推导特征，具备较高候选价值。
虽有 metadata.image_paths 为空的登记问题，但已有可用图像文件落库，未造成核心资产缺失。
选项文本存在重复会降低基于选项的校验价值，但不影响题干、图像与标准答案共同恢复题意。

normalization_record.normalized_question_text:
Thread around a cylinder

One end of a thread is tied into a loop of length $L>2 \pi R$, and a cylinder of radius $R$ is put through the loop. The coefficient of friction between the thread and the cylinder is $\mu$. The free end of the thread is being pulled parallel to the axis of the cylinder (as shown by the arrow in the photo) while keeping the cylinder at rest. If the length of the loop is longer than a critical value, $L>L_{0}$, the loop can slide along the cylinder without changing its shape; otherwise the friction "locks" it into place and increasing the pulling force would eventually just break the thread. Find this critical value $L_{0}$. The weight of the thread is to be neglected; the thread will not twist when being pulled.

It might be useful to know that

$$
2 \int \sqrt{1+x^{2}} \, \mathrm{d}x = x \sqrt{1+x^{2}} + \operatorname{arcsinh} x,
$$

where $\operatorname{arcsinh} x \equiv \ln \left(x+\sqrt{1+x^{2}}\right)$.
normalization_notes: Removed the inline image placeholder token `<image_1>` as formatting noise.
Preserved the photo reference because the problem explicitly refers to the arrow/image.
Set `requires_image=true` and `cleaning_path=multimodal_full` because `force_requires_image=true` and the prompt depends on the depicted setup.
Preserved the original answer text and all provided options, including duplicate equivalent choices.

The image is simple but semantically useful: it clearly shows a thread around a cylinder and the pull direction parallel to the cylinder axis, matching the text. Together they are understandable enough for annotation.

Because the text mostly carries the setup this is recoverable, yet the multimodal linkage risk and minor alignment conflict make review safer than an automatic pass.

Thread around a cylinder

One end of a thread is tied into a loop of length $L>2 \pi R$, and a cylinder of radius $R$ is put through the loop. The coefficient of friction between the thread and the cylinder is $\mu$. The free end of the thread is being pulled parallel to the axis of the cylinder (as shown by the arrow in the photo) while keeping the cylinder at rest. If the length of the loop is longer than a critical value, $L>L_{0}$, the loop can slide along the cylinder without changing its shape; otherwise the friction "locks" it into place and increasing the pulling force would eventually just break the thread. Find this critical value $L_{0}$.

The weight of the thread is to be neglected; the thread will not twist when being pulled.

It might be useful to know that

$$
2 \int \sqrt{1+x^{2}} \, \mathrm{d}x = x \sqrt{1+x^{2}} + \operatorname{arcsinh} x,
$$

where $\operatorname{arcsinh} x \equiv \ln \left(x+\sqrt{1+x^{2}}\right)$.

open_ended_problem_variants.title: Dataset Open Question
